nodes.label: Anti-AI-Look Principle (Two-Second Glance Test)
nodes.rationale: If a stranger could glance at the page for two seconds and say 'an AI made this', the build has failed regardless of working code. Distinctiveness and restraint are the job; every rule in the file exists to prevent the default vibe-coded look.

nodes.label: Hard Bans
nodes.rationale: Bans the fingerprints of vibe-coded sites: purple/violet primary colors, purple-blue gradients, gradient-filled headline words, meaningless stat blocks, emoji in headings, 'Why Choose [Brand]?' sections, default glassmorphism, pill-badge clutter, centered-everything layouts, raw Inter/system type, and drop shadows on everything. An explicit brief overrides a ban but must be confirmed as intentional.

nodes.label: Follow User References First
nodes.rationale: User-provided references override all guidance except the hard bans; pull actual hex values and the reference's signature move rather than 'improving' toward a generic look.

nodes.label: Typography Standards (Display + Body Pairing)
nodes.rationale: Type carries the personality; pair a characterful display face with a legible body face and a deliberate scale. Named starter palettes include Fraunces/Libre Caslon + Inter Tight (editorial), Space Grotesk + IBM Plex Sans (technical), Bricolage Grotesque + Source Serif (warm), Geist/Satoshi + Newsreader (premium).

nodes.label: Color Discipline (4-6 Named Values, One Accent)
nodes.rationale: Palette must derive from the subject or reference: a confident neutral base plus one disciplined accent, WCAG AA contrast, energy from composition and type rather than gradients.

nodes.label: Layout and Composition (Hero as Thesis)
nodes.rationale: The hero should open with the most characteristic thing about the subject, not a centered slogan + buttons + stat row. Use asymmetry, editorial grids, varied section rhythm; structural devices like numbered steps must encode something true.

nodes.label: Motion Restraint
nodes.rationale: Motion only where it serves the subject; one orchestrated moment beats scattered effects because excess animation reads as AI-generated. Always respect prefers-reduced-motion.

nodes.label: Responsive Mobile-First Requirement
nodes.rationale: Non-negotiable: build mobile-first, test at 375/768/1024/1440px, tap targets >= 44px, working mobile navigation, no horizontal scroll.

nodes.label: Quality Floor (Accessibility Baseline)
nodes.rationale: Built in silently: visible focus states, semantic HTML, alt text, labelled form controls, prefers-reduced-motion, and verified CSS specificity so spacing actually applies.

nodes.label: Vibe-Code Checklist
nodes.rationale: Post-build review gate: every item (purple dominance, gradients, gradient headlines, stat rows, emoji headings, generic SaaS sections, single centered column, default Inter, frosted-glass cards, broken mobile) must be NO before the build is finished. Includes Chanel's rule: remove one thing that isn't earning its place.
